description: Rank correlation study: comparing our v0.4 framework's grades against public ratings from BeZero, Calyx Global, and Sylvera for projects where multi-rater data is public.
primary_source: Perspectives Climate Group / Carbon Market Watch 2023 report: 'Assessing and comparing carbon credit rating agencies' (Freiburg, 11.09.2023), Table 20 (Overlapping ratings compared on normative scale). All agency ratings as of 02 June 2023.
important_caveats: Agency ratings are a snapshot as of 02 June 2023. Some projects have moved since (e.g. Kariba BBB->D, later delisted in Oct 2023).
Calyx NZM scale is a 5-point reduction of their 10-point internal scale for the Net Zero Marketplace.
Sylvera NZM scale is a 3-point tier reduction of their 8-point internal scale for the Net Zero Marketplace.
Our v0.4 per-dimension scores are author judgment from public project documentation, not a formal rating.
n=6 is small for statistical correlation. Report correlations with confidence intervals and treat as directional evidence not as a definitive benchmark.
The CMW report did not include a framework like ours for comparison; we are adding our v0.4 grades to their multi-rater table.

scale_mappings.comment: Monotonic integer mappings for rank correlation. Higher = better quality on every scale.

projects.name: Ecomapua Amazon REDD+
projects.notes: Most-disagreed project in the CMW dataset. Sylvera Tier 1 vs BeZero C vs Calyx D is nearly maximal inter-agency disagreement.

projects.name: Keo Seima Wildlife Sanctuary
projects.notes: WCS-run, CCB Gold, Indigenous Community Land Titles. BeZero A + Sylvera Tier 1 on watch vs Calyx E is extreme disagreement. Our BBB falls between the extremes.

projects.name: Mai Ndombe REDD+
projects.notes: Wildlife Works, world's largest VCS REDD+, CCB Gold. Additionality extensively criticized (REDD-Monitor 2017).

projects.name: Envira Amazonia Project
projects.notes: JRA Agropecuaria (commercial developer); 39,300 ha in Acre, Brazil. BeZero BBB is the agency outlier; Calyx E and Sylvera Tier 2 are harsher.

projects.name: Luangwa Community Forests Project
projects.notes: Community-based REDD+ program. BioCarbon Partners developer. Stronger additionality argument than industrial REDD+ but low MRV.

projects.name: Guanaré Forest Restoration Project
projects.notes: Commercial eucalyptus plantation classified as ARR. Our BBB is the outlier here; all 3 agencies rate it low. Likely driven by: (a) commercial plantation additionality concerns the v0.4 rubric may be too generous on, (b) biodiversity/community co-benefit concerns that v0.4's safeguards-gate doesn't catch because co_benefits is only a gate for documented harm, not moderate weakness.

anchor_projects.name: Climeworks Orca (DACCS Iceland)
anchor_projects.source: BeZero first-ever AAA, confirmed in climeworks.com news and multiple industry publications (2023).
anchor_projects.note: Full agreement. BeZero describes it as the most likely tonne-for-tonne removal they rate.

anchor_projects.name: Kariba REDD+
anchor_projects.source: BeZero downgraded BBB -> rating watch (Jan 2023) -> D (Oct 2023), then delisted entirely.
anchor_projects.note: Directional agreement: both rate at the bottom of their respective scales. Sylvera also documented 75-90% baseline overstatement and ~33% carbon stock overstatement, but did not publish a letter grade.
